data.text: The new electric vehicle from Tesla set records this quarter with 500,000 deliveries worldwide. Battery technology improvements reduced costs by 30% while increasing range to 400 miles. Critics point to quality control issues and competition from Chinese manufacturers like BYD.

data.text:
# Text Analysis Report

## Sentiment
neutral (confidence: 0.75)
The text contains both positive elements (record-breaking deliveries of 500,000 vehicles, 30% cost reduction, increased range to 400 miles) and negative elements (quality control issues, competition concerns). These contrasting aspects balance each other out, making the overall sentiment neutral rather than clearly positive or negative.

## Keywords
keywords: ```

 • item #1:

# keyword: Tesla

# relevance: 0.95

 • item #2:

# keyword: electric vehicle

# relevance: 0.9

 • item #3:

# keyword: deliveries

# relevance: 0.75

 • item #4:

# keyword: battery technology

# relevance: 0.85

 • item #5:

# keyword: cost reduction

# relevance: 0.7

 • item #6:

# keyword: range

# relevance: 0.75

 • item #7:

# keyword: quality control

# relevance: 0.65

 • item #8:

# keyword: Chinese manufacturers

# relevance: 0.6

 • item #9:

# keyword: BYD

# relevance: 0.7

 • item #10:

# keyword: competition

# relevance: 0.65

```

execution_data.rendered_user_prompt:
Analyze the sentiment of the following text. Determine if it is positive, negative, or neutral:

<text>
The new electric vehicle from Tesla set records this quarter with 500,000 deliveries worldwide. Battery technology improvements reduced costs by 30% while increasing range to 400 miles. Critics point to quality control issues and competition from Chinese manufacturers like BYD.
</text>

---
The instance we want to generate will be for the following class:
class Sentiment(StructuredContent):
    """Sentiment analysis result"""
    sentiment: Literal[
        "positive",
        "negative",
        "neutral",
    ]  # The detected sentiment
    confidence: float  # Confidence score 0-1
    explanation: str  # Why this sentiment was detected

DO NOT create information.
If some information is not present for an attribute, output the default value or None according to the attribute definition.

execution_data.rendered_user_prompt:
Extract the most relevant keywords from the following text:

<text>
The new electric vehicle from Tesla set records this quarter with 500,000 deliveries worldwide. Battery technology improvements reduced costs by 30% while increasing range to 400 miles. Critics point to quality control issues and competition from Chinese manufacturers like BYD.
</text>

---
The instance we want to generate will be for the following class:
class Keyword(StructuredContent):
    """An extracted keyword with relevance score"""
    keyword: str  # The keyword text
    relevance: float  # Relevance score 0-1

DO NOT create information.
If some information is not present for an attribute, output the default value or None according to the attribute definition.
